seoTitle: How to Add a CNAME Record in Cloudflare (2026)
metaDescription: Create or edit a CNAME record in Cloudflare to alias a subdomain to another destination, and choose the right proxy status for your setup.
intro: CNAME records let you point a subdomain, like www or blog, at another domain rather than an IP address, which is common when using third-party platforms. This guide covers creating and editing CNAME records in Cloudflare's DNS panel.

steps.heading: Open the DNS Records grid
steps.body: Select your domain in Cloudflare and open the DNS Records panel from the sidebar.

steps.heading: Click Add Record
steps.body: Click '+ Add Record' to open the field configuration panel.

steps.heading: Set the type to CNAME
steps.body: Change the Type dropdown to select the CNAME format.

steps.heading: Enter your subdomain and target
steps.body: Type your subdomain handle, such as 'www' or 'blog', and match it to your external destination domain name.

steps.heading: Set the proxy status and save
steps.body: Toggle between proxied (orange cloud, for edge speed and security) or DNS Only (gray cloud, for direct routing), then save to deploy the record.

faq.question: Can I use a CNAME record for my root domain?
faq.answer: Generally no, most DNS standards restrict CNAME records to subdomains rather than the apex root; use an A record for the root domain instead.

faq.question: What's the benefit of proxying a CNAME record?
faq.answer: Proxying enables Cloudflare's caching, DDoS protection, and SSL features for that subdomain's traffic.

faq.question: Why would I need to edit an existing CNAME record?
faq.answer: You might need to point a subdomain at a different destination, such as when switching third-party service providers.

faq.question: How do I verify a CNAME record is resolving correctly?
faq.answer: You can check it directly in the DNS Records grid or use an external DNS lookup tool to confirm it points to the expected destination.
